rationale: The US-Iran peace deal announcement is the dominant story but remains largely rhetorical — signing not yet confirmed as enacted — keeping energy price relief as the main indirect benefit for Poland; no new qualitative change from last cycle.

top_events.title: US-Iran announce ceasefire/peace deal; oil prices fall
top_events.summary: Oil prices sliding on the deal announcement could ease energy costs for Polish consumers and industry, but the deal is not yet signed/enacted — continuation of last cycle's baseline.

top_events.title: Stock markets soar on US-Iran deal confirmation
top_events.summary: Rising global markets indirectly benefit Polish financial markets and pension funds, but this is a continuation of the trend established last cycle with no new qualitative leap.

top_events.title: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
top_events.summary: A human-interest story from the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war with no direct impact on Polish daily life or security situation.
